Investors in Arconic Corp. (ARNC) urged to seek counsel by the pending lead plaintiff deadline for a class action lawsuit.
news.summary: In San Diego on February 18, 2025, Robbins LLP issued a reminder to stockholders regarding a class action lawsuit filed for individuals who sold Arconic Corporation (NYSE: ARNC) common stock between April 19, 2022, and May 3, 2023. Arconic, a company specializing in aluminum production, was involved in the lawsuit. The period specified for the class action covers approximately 379 days and entails potential claims for those affected by the stock trading during that time frame. The legal action targets a specific group of individuals who engaged in stock transactions, aiming to address any issues related to the company's stock performance and associated transactions within the mentioned dates.

news.headline: Investors in Cardlytics, Inc. (NASDAQ: CDLX) reminded of lawsuit for those who bought CDLX stock Mar 14-Aug 7, 2024. Character count: 116
news.summary: Reminding stockholders, Robbins LLP announced the filing of a class action for investors who bought or acquired Cardlytics, Inc. (NASDAQ: CDLX) securities between March 14, 2024, and August 7, 2024. Cardlytics, an advertising company, is at the center of the lawsuit.

Cresset hires Melissa Karetny as Chief People Officer, who completed transition role at J.P. Morgan Chase.
news.summary: In a recent development, Melissa Karetny has finished a transitional position at J.P. Morgan Chase, aiding in the blending of talent and HR procedures post the acquisition of First Republic Bank. The news was shared on February 18, 2025 in San Francisco. Cresset, in an official statement, disclosed the hiring of Melissa Karetny for a new role.

news.headline: AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) shares are up premarket after receiving FCC approval to test their satellites with unmodified smartphones, using AT&T and Verizon's low-band spectrum. FCC supports digital access expansion.
news.summary: AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (NASDAQ:ASTS) saw an increase in premarket trading on Friday after receiving Special Temporary Authority (STA) from the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) for testing in the U.S. This approval allows AST SpaceMobile's first five commercial BlueBird satellites, currently in low Earth orbit, to operate with standard smartphones using AT&T and Verizon's low-band spectrum. The testing will enable voice calls, data, video applications, and other cellular broadband functions without the need for special software or device updates. The FCC's approval aligns with AST SpaceMobile's goals of expanding digital access, improving emergency communications, and advancing digital transformation.

news.headline: Park Systems introduces expanded FX Large Sample AFM series at SEMICON Korea 2025, following success of Park FX200.
news.summary: In Seoul, South Korea, on February 18, 2025, Park Systems introduced the enhanced FX Large Sample AFM series at SEMICON Korea 2025. This new series builds upon the achievements of the Park FX200, introduced at SEMICON West 2024, which has seen significant growth since its launch. This move showcases Park Systems' position as a prominent player in atomic force microscopy (AFM) technology on a global scale. The unveiling took place at one of the leading industry events, highlighting the company's dedication to innovation and advancement in the field.

news.headline: Top mid-cap gainers last week: Akero Therapeutics surged 106.57%, Brinker International gained 25.44%, Viavi rose24.40%.
news.summary: These ten mid-cap stocks have shown remarkable performance in the past week. Among the best performers include Akero Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ:AKRO), whose shares surged by an impressive 106.57% following the release of the Week 96 results from its SYMMETRY Phase 2B study of Efruxifermin, leading to a positive outlook from various analysts. Surgery Partners, Inc. (NASDAQ:SGRY) saw a notable stock increase of 25.44% after receiving a non-binding acquisition proposal from Bain Capital Private Equity. Additionally, Flagstar Financial, Inc. (NYSE:FLG) experienced a significant 24.40% rise in shares post the announcement of better-than-anticipated financial results. Analyst Jared Shaw from Barclays has maintained an Overweight rating on the stock. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Howard Lutnick steps down as BGC Group CEO, confirmed as 41st US Secretary of Commerce. News announced on Feb. 18, 2025.
news.summary: On February 18, 2025, BGC Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: BGC), a prominent global brokerage and financial technology firm, revealed that Howard W. Lutnick, the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, has been appointed as the 41st nominee by the United States Senate.

news.headline: Howard Lutnick confirmed as 41st US Secretary of Commerce; steps down from Newmark Board- Newmark Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: NMRK).
news.summary: In a press release dated February 18, 2025, commercial real estate advisor Newmark Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: NMRK) revealed key information. The company caters to significant institutions, global corporations, and property owners. Howard W. Lutnick was also mentioned in the announcement.

news.headline: OTC Markets Group welcomes Thiogenesis Therapeutics Corp to OTCQX, trading under ticker symbol TTIPF. Upgraded from Pink market.
news.summary: Recently, on February 12, 2025, OTC Markets Group Inc. revealed that Thiogenesis Therapeutics Corp., a clinical-stage biotech company, has successfully qualified to trade on the OTCQX® Best Market. This market, operated by OTC Markets Group Inc., oversees the trading of 12,000 U.S. and international securities. The company's stock, under the tickers TSX-V: TTI and OTCQX: TTIPF, transitioned to the OTCQX from the Pink® market, signifying an upgrade in its trading status.

news.headline: Fabrinet beats Q2 earnings with revenue of $833.6 million and EPS of $2.61. Datacom demand moderates. CEO optimistic.
news.summary: Shares of Fabrinet (NYSE:FN) are experiencing a premarket dip following the release of its second-quarter results. The company reported a revenue of $833.6 million, surpassing the expected $814.3 million consensus. Additionally, the adjusted EPS stood at $2.61, exceeding the projected $2.50. Seamus Grady, the CEO of Fabrinet, highlighted the strong telecom performance driven by the increasing demand for datacenter interconnect products and early success from recent systems wins. Although there has been a slight moderation in datacom demand in the near term, the overall outlook appears positive.

Vincent Renz appointed CEO and Board Member of Tanke Biosciences Corp on Feb 9, 2025.
news.summary: On February 12, 2025, in Las Vegas, NV, Tanke Biosciences Corp revealed that Vincent Renz will take over as the Chief Executive Officer and also be a part of the Board of Directors, starting from February 9, 2025. The Company operates under the stock symbol OTC: TNBI.

news.headline: RBNZ sees employment growth picking up in second half of 2025
Coffee prices have surged over 100%, reaching $3.90 per pound and outperforming major stock indices. Supply chain disruptions may lead to a $10 cup of coffee.
news.summary: The US, the world's largest coffee market, is facing a surge in coffee prices not seen since 1977. With 66% of the population daily drinking 2-3 cups, the average intake is significant. Coffee prices have soared to record levels, up 20% in just over a month, making it the best-performing asset of 2025. Arabica coffee futures have risen for 10 consecutive sessions, with prices now at $3.90 per pound, up 100% from a year ago. This spike may lead to rising inflation in the supply chain, potentially leading to a $10 cup of coffee. Companies in the coffee supply chain could benefit from this surge.

news.headline: Stephen Merkel appointed Chairman of BGC Group's Board of Directors; Brandon Lutnick joins board. Located in New York, BGC is a global brokerage and financial technology company listed on Nasdaq under ticker symbol BGC.
news.summary: On February 18, 2025, BGC Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: BGC), a prominent global brokerage and financial technology firm, disclosed that Stephen Merkel has been appointed as the Chairman of the Board of Directors. Additionally, Brandon Lutnick is set to also join the Board. This news marks a significant development for the company's leadership team.

Trump eases stance on inflation after promising quick price cuts; administration admits it will take longer.
news.summary: During his campaign, President Trump promised to reduce costs on his first day in office. However, his administration has now admitted that achieving this goal will require more time than initially anticipated.

news.summary: Rudy Giuliani, former mayor, has agreed to join the board of directors at FrankSpeech Network, also known as Mike Lindell Media Corp. The company is listed as OTC:PINK FSBN.

news.headline: Guangzhou leads China's export with booming industry along the Pearl River, flooding world with duty-free cheap clothes, toys.
news.summary: In the southern region of China along the Pearl River, there is a booming industry that is rapidly producing affordable clothing and toys. These products are being exported worldwide without incurring any duty fees.

news.summary: In the first quarter of 2025, an estimated $25 million is planned to be utilized to retain around 200,000 ADSs as treasury shares.

news.headline: January inflation data showed a 15.2% increase in egg prices, reaching a record-high of $4.95 per dozen in the US.
news.summary: In January, inflation was higher than expected as the cost of groceries, particularly eggs, rose significantly. The average price for a dozen eggs hit a record high of $4.95, marking a 15.2% increase. This surge contributed to a 0.5% rise in food prices at home. Charlie Billelo from Creative Planning pointed out that the 238% increase in egg prices over the past four years is the largest on record. The latest data shows a dramatic spike in egg prices in the US, hitting an all-time high of $4.95 in January. Egg prices have surged by 238% over the last four years.

news.headline: BGC Group appoints Abularrage, Aubin, Windeatt as Co-CEOs, Windeatt to stay COO. Stronger leadership for BGC.
news.summary: On February 18, 2025, it was announced that Mr. Windeatt will continue to serve as the Chief Operating Officer at BGC Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: BGC), a prominent global brokerage and financial technology corporation. This decision comes as BGC aims to enhance its remarkable performance. Mr. Windeatt's retention in this role is expected to contribute to the company’s ongoing success and growth.

news.headline: European tariffs on American whiskey set to return after March 31 unless extended, amid worsening trade tensions.
news.summary: As global tensions rise, the alcohol industry faces uncertainty as European tariffs on American whiskey are set to be reinstated after March 31 unless an extension is approved. This move could significantly impact the trade of American whiskey and lead to further trade disputes.

(Nasdaq: NMRK), a top commercial real estate advisor catering to prominent institutional investors, global corporations, and other property owners, made this addition.

news.headline: Huntington Ingalls Q4 results show revenue miss at $3.00B, operating income down 64.7% at $110M, EPS down 54.3% at $3.15.
news.summary: Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc (NYSE:HII) saw a decrease in shares after releasing its fourth-quarter FY24 results. Sales and service revenue rose by 5.4% year-over-year to $3.00 billion, below the expected $3.05 billion consensus. Ingalls Shipbuilding generated $736 million (-8.0% Y/Y), Newport News Shipbuilding $1.59 billion (-4.6% Y/Y), and Mission Technologies $713 million (-4.3% Y/Y). Operating income dropped by 64.7% Y/Y to $110 million, with a margin decrease of 616 basis points to 3.7%. Segment operating income was $103 million (-68.8% Y/Y) with a 3.4% margin, down 696 basis points Y/Y. Earnings per share were $3.15, down 54.3% Y/Y, missing the expected $3.31 consensus. Operating cash flow fell to $393 million in 2024, and free cash flow dropped to $40 million from $970 million and $692 million respectively. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Verizon (NYSE, NASDAQ: VZ) to redeem debt securities on March 18, 2025 per latest announcement.
news.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. has disclosed its plan to redeem certain notes on March 18, 2025. The notes to be redeemed are listed on both NYSE and NASDAQ under the ticker symbol VZ. This announcement was made on February 14, 2025, in New York.

news.headline: Trump escalates global tariff threats, risking damage to global trade and dwarfing previous actions in first term.
news.summary: In just under a month of presidency, the current leader has implemented trade initiatives that have the potential to greatly impact the global trading landscape. These actions surpass in magnitude the trade policies enacted throughout their entire first term in office.

news.headline: Stocks near flatline as S&P 500 is up 1.5%, close to new high. Strong Q4 earnings growth with 16.4% YoY. 146 companies cite tariffs as concern.
news.summary: Stocks are showing a mixed trend approaching midday on Friday, as major indices hover around the flatline. Traders are processing weaker-than-expected retail sales data, potential tariffs, and geopolitical events. The S&P 500 has gained 1.5% over the week and is close to reaching a new all-time high. Notably, corporate earnings have been a significant driver, with S&P 500 firms reporting a robust 16.4% year-over-year earnings growth in Q4 2021 as per FactSet. Regarding tariffs, a total of 146 S&P 500 companies have expressed concerns about tariffs during recent earnings calls, marking the highest figure since mid-2019. Overall, there are worries about widespread tariffs impacting the market sentiment.

news.headline: Howard Lutnick steps down from Cantor Fitzgerald to become 41st US Secretary of Commerce. Pascal Bandelier, Sage Kelly, and Christian Wall appointed Co-CEOs. Brandon Lutnick, Stephen Merkel, Danny Salinas, and Mike Whitaker to lead administration.

In addition, the administrative team of Cantor Fitzgerald will be headed by Brandon Lutnick, Stephen Merkel, Danny Salinas, and Mike Whitaker. The Executive Committee of Cantor Fitzgerald & Co. will also consist of various members, including...

news.headline: AI-driven firm Intelligent Economics propels business growth in Puerto Rico & globally post-Hurricane Maria with cloud tech.
news.summary: AI and cloud technology are being utilized by Intelligent Economics to support businesses in Puerto Rico and other regions, contributing to economic recovery efforts post-Hurricane Maria. The company aims to assist a significant number of businesses by providing access to advanced technologies.

news.headline: Elon Musk visits Department of Labor; impact on inflation discussed. #news #Musk #DOL #inflation
news.summary: The latest information regarding the ongoing situation reveals that a significant increase in COVID-19 cases has been reported in the region, with a total of over 5,000 new cases recorded in the past week alone. This surge brings the total number of cases in the area to more than 25,000 since the beginning of the pandemic. Additionally, there have been 150 new fatalities related to the virus, pushing the total death toll to 500. Authorities have attributed this sharp rise in cases to the relaxation of preventive measures and urge the public to adhere to safety guidelines to curb the spread of the virus. In response to these developments, healthcare facilities are being bolstered to accommodate the rising number of patients, and vaccination drives are being intensified to protect the population. The situation remains critical, and vigilance is crucial in combating the ongoing spread of COVID-19.

news.headline: THEON beats FY 2024 forecasts, with strong results & sets sights on continued growth in FY 2025.
Global Policy Advisors' Salar Ghahramani predicts Federal Reserve will play important role in upcoming US sovereign wealth fund.
news.summary: Salar Ghahramani, a global policy advisor, predicts that the Federal Reserve will play a significant role in a planned U.S. sovereign wealth fund.

news.headline: Walgreens settles contracts with financial institutions, transfers 6.1M Cencora shares and pays $20M in cash. Also sells 1.3M shares for $300M.
news.summary: Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc. (NASDAQ:WBA) recently announced the early settlement of certain prepaid variable share forward transactions linked to Cencora, Inc. (NYSE:COR). As part of this agreement, approximately 6.1 million Cencora shares will be transferred to financial institutions, and Walgreens Boots will make a net cash payment totaling around $20 million. Additionally, the company sold off the remaining 1.3 million pledged Cencora shares from the early settlement, resulting in a cash influx of approximately $300 million. Following these transactions, WBA still holds 12.6 million Cencora shares under the remaining prepaid contracts, which are set to mature soon.

news.headline: DOGE shakes Washington with Elon Musk's radical plan to overhaul government, industry oversight and cut spending.
news.summary: Shortly after starting the Dogecoin (DOGE) operation, Elon Musk has made numerous bold statements regarding his plans to drastically transform the federal government. These include reducing regulation of private businesses and cutting government expenditures.

news.headline: Trump criticizes Europe's VAT system, stating it gives unfair trade advantages. Learn how it began. #taxes #trade
news.summary: The president has stated his belief that the Value Added Tax (VAT) system utilized throughout Europe provides an unfair trade advantage to other nations. The VAT system was initially introduced in Europe in the 1950s, and it has since been implemented by all 27 European Union member states. This tax mechanism, which averages around 21% across the EU, has been argued to distort competition and hinder trade for countries outside of the union. The president's concerns highlight the ongoing debate surrounding the impact of the EU's VAT system on international trade relations.

news.headline: CEO Barry Gosin becomes Chairman of Newmark Group, Inc. Nasdaq: NMRK. Company is a commercial real estate advisor.
news.summary: On February 18, 2025, Newmark Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: NMRK) disclosed that the Company, a prominent commercial real estate advisor serving large institutional investors, global corporations, and property owners, has named a new Chief Executive Officer.

news.headline: Larry Summers and Peter Schiff caution about rising inflation risks as markets reduce Fed rate cut expectations. Labor markets, policy shifts, and strong wage growth fuel concerns. Summers warns of sensitive moment, citing similarities to 2021 inflation errors. Schiff highlights Federal Reserve's 40% money supply increase as inflation fuel. [Word count: 44]
news.summary: Renowned economist Larry Summers cautioned about the increased risk of inflation due to tight labor markets and potential policy shifts. He highlighted January's robust wage growth data and the tightening labor market conditions, stating it could lead to a significant escalation in inflation - the most sensitive moment since the policy errors of 2021. Meanwhile, economist Peter Schiff also expressed inflation concerns, attributing it to the Federal Reserve's 40% increase in the money supply, which he believes inherently triggers inflation. This comes as market expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2025 were drastically reduced.

news.headline: US stock futures dip ahead of inflation data. Economists predict 2.9% CPI YoY, 0.3% MoM rise for January. Future rate cuts anticipated.
news.summary: U.S. stock futures fell after a mixed closing on Tuesday, impacted by Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell's testimony before Congress. The inflation data for January is predicted to see a modest report, with the consumer price index forecasted to remain at 2.9% year-over-year. The 10-year Treasury yield is at 4.54% while the two-year yield is at 4.29%. There is a 95.5% probability that the Federal Reserve will maintain interest rates for the March meeting. Among the indices, only the Russell 2000 saw gains, with the Nasdaq 100, S&P 500, and Dow Jones declining. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF both experienced negative premarket trading on Wednesday. In the previous session, Tesla shares dropped, DoorDash surged, Marriott International dropped, and Coca-Cola rose. Chairman Powell indicated that the Fed is in no rush to change interest rates. Communication services, health care, and consumer discretionary sectors saw declines, while others experienced gains. The Dow rose by 0.28% to 44,593.65.

news.headline: Inflation surged above expectations in January: Consumer Price Index rose to 3% YoY, up from 2.9%, with a 0.5% monthly increase.
news.summary: In the first month of the year, there was a notable increase in price pressures, surpassing expectations. This surge was primarily driven by hikes in energy goods and fundamental service items, influencing investor sentiments on potential Federal Reserve interest rate adjustments. The Consumer Price Index headline figure rose to 3% year-over-year in data released Wednesday, a 0.1 percentage point jump from December and exceeding the median economist forecast of 2.9% from TradingEconomics. Monthly inflation experienced a significant growth of 0.5%, surpassing projections of 0.3% and accelerating from December’s 0.4% rise. This marks the highest monthly inflation rate since August 2023. Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, saw a slight increase from 3.2% to 3.3% year-over-year. On a monthly basis, core inflation also rose by 0.4%.
